mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the past 6 months indicates rising inflation expectations, which would lead to higher interest rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 60-day forward returns in high-inflation periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decline in the unemployment rate over the past 3 months signals a tightening labor market, which would lead to higher wages and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) over the past 6 months indicates rising production capacity, which would lead to higher economic growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or 60-day forward returns in high-growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the RSAFS (Retail Sales) over the past 3 months, combined with a 0.5% decline in the unemployment rate over the same period, signals rising consumer spending and a tightening labor market, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or 20-day forward returns in high-spending and tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the federal funds rate over the past 3 months, combined with a 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the same period, signals a tightening monetary cycle and rising inflation expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 60-day forward returns in tightening monetary and high-inflation periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% decline in the unemployment rate over the past 6 months, combined with a 2% increase in the average hourly earnings over the same period, signals a tightening labor market and rising wages, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or 20-day forward returns in tightening labor market and high-wage periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the housing starts (HOUST) over the past 6 months, combined with a 1% increase in the retail sales (RSAFS) over the same period, signals rising consumer spending and housing demand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or 60-day forward returns in high-spending and high-housing demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
